title: To the Husbandmen (Colonos) of the Syracusan Patrimony.

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: I would have you know that we have arranged for you to be put under the care of our guardian (defensoris).

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And accordingly we order you to obey him without any reluctance in what he may see fit to do, and enjoin on you to be done, for the advantage of the Church.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: We have given him such power as to enable him to inflict strict punishment on those who may attempt to be disobedient or contumacious.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And we have likewise charged him that he delay not with instant attention to recover to ecclesiastical jurisdiction any slaves who are in hiding outside their limits, or any one by whom boundaries have been invaded.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For know that he has been warned on his peril, that he presume not ever under any kind of excuse to do any wrong or robbery in regard to what belongs to others.
